mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving robust aggregate demand that lifts equity valuations over a quarter-long horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 70% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and force rapid equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during high inflation periods yield positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no directional bias when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% while inflation remains above 3.0%, real rates turn sharply positive, increasing discount rates and liquidity constraints that suppress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of high-rate high-inflation observations, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for term premium movements.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate revenue expectations, creating a persistent upward drift in equity prices over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales reveals underlying demand resilience that markets initially underprice, generating positive short-term alpha as earnings revisions catch up.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth exceeds RSAFS growth by > 0.5%
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict 5-day returns with statistical significance, or the signal reverses direction when consumer confidence indices drop below 80.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial output combined with rising unemployment during economic contractions signals severe demand destruction and capacity utilization drops, forcing broad equity sell-offs over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Concurrent industrial decline and unemployment increases produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of historical recession episodes, or the mechanism loses predictive power when fiscal stimulus is announced.
